title: Screen Clearing Mnemonics
text: Screen Clearing Mnemonics The mnemonics for clearing all or parts of the screen only need to be defined for a display device, since printers do not have a screen to clear. Arguments Table 1. Mnemonics for Clearing the Screen Mnemonic Explanation clear_screen Clear the entire screen. erase_line Erase part of line. erase_line from (%h,%v) to (%x,%y) Erase part of line. erase_line from (%h,%v) Erase % m or % p positions. Parameters % h ,% v —horizontal and vertical starting coordinates. % x ,% y —horizontal and vertical ending coordinates. Description The clear_screen mnemonic is generally followed by a cursor function. When interpreting erase_line , if the output sequence does not include the %p , %x, or %m parameters (the end position), Uniface assumes that the device is not able to erase partial lines. Uniface then only uses this mnemonic if it is necessary to clear an entire line. It writes spaces if a partial line erase is needed. The parameters for erase_line support two different ways to erase a part of a line: either from a certain position ( %h, %v ) to a certain position ( %x, %y ), or from a certain position ( %h, %v ) over a certain number of positions (either %m or %p ). Uniface assumes that after erase_line , the cursor is positioned at the last cleared position. If the device does not do this, use the %p parameter instead of %m . If the %p parameter is included in the output sequence, Uniface uses the cursor position after the erase_line to reposition the cursor. Example: Examples The following example shows the definition for the standard VT display: ^027[2J ^CLEAR_SCREEN Copy The following example shows the definition for the standard VT340G display: ’S(E)T’ ^CLEAR_SCREEN Copy The following example shows the definition for the standard Sun display: ^027[1;1H^027[J ^CLEAR_SCREEN Copy The following example shows the definition for the standard VT display: ^027[%pX ^ERASE_LINE Copy The following example shows the definition for the standard Sun display: ^027[K ^ERASE_LINE Copy Related Links Parameters in the Output Sequence Some mnemonics include parameters in the output sequence that can be used to determine layout and formatting information, such as horizontal and vertical coordinates, numeric base zero (decimal, octal, or hexadecimal), zero padding, and so on. The output sequence can even include expressions that use the parameters incalculation, for example, to increment values. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
